channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 25, 13:10 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 25, 10:06 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 25, 09:35 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Warsaw, Poland (WAW)
item.description: Mar 25, 12:53 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 25, 12:43 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 25, 12:29 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with network performance in Warsaw, Poland (WAW)....

item.title: The Download: reawakening frozen brains, and the AI Hype Index returns
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain  L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits in a vat at a storage...

item.title: FCC Bans New Routers Made Outside the US Over National Security Risks
item.description: The ban aligns with a White House determination that all routers produced abroad are a threat to national security. The post FCC Bans New Routers Made Outside the US Over National Security Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Network Analytics Issues
item.description: Mar 25, 12:32 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue causing Spectrum Analytics to not displaying correctly within the dashboard. Service traffic and protection remain unaffected as this is a reporting-only delay.

item.title: RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Day 2)
item.description: A summary of the announcements made by vendors on the second day of the RSAC 2026 Conference. The post RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Day 2) appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Kill Chain Is Obsolete When Your AI Agent Is the Threat
item.description: In September 2025, Anthropic disclosed that a state-sponsored threat actor used an AI coding agent to execute an autonomous cyber espionage campaign against 30 global targets. The AI handled 80-90% of tactical operations on its own, performing reconnaissance, writing exploit code, and attempting...

item.title: From Trivy to Broad OSS Compromise: TeamPCP Hits Docker Hub, VS Code, PyPI
item.description: The hackers compromised GitHub Action tags, then shifted to NPM, Docker Hub, VS Code, and PyPI, and teamed with Lapsus$. The post From Trivy to Broad OSS Compromise: TeamPCP Hits Docker Hub, VS Code, PyPI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Russian Hacker Sentenced to 2 Years for TA551 Botnet-Driven Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) said a Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison for managing a botnet that was used to launch ransomware attacks against U.S. companies. Ilya Angelov, 40, of Tolyatti, Russia, was also fined $100,000. Angelov, who went by the online aliases...

item.title: Agentic commerce runs on truth and context
item.description: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of links, the agent assembles an itinerary and executes the purchase. That shift, from assistance to...

item.title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to an active device code phishing campaign that's targeting Microsoft 365 identities across more than 340 organizations in the U.S., Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and Germany. The activity, per Huntress, was first spotted on February 19, 2026, with...

item.title: Network Error Logging Issues
item.description: Mar 25, 11:29 UTCIdentified - As the fix is deployed, customers may notice an increase in NEL reports as data that was previously not forwarded is correctly reported.Mar 25, 09:20 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue causing intermittent data gaps within Network Error Logging...

item.title: US Prisons Russian Access Broker for Aiding Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Aleksei Volkov has been sentenced to 81 months in prison for his role in Yanluowang ransomware attacks. The post US Prisons Russian Access Broker for Aiding Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The AI Hype Index: AI goes to war
item.description: AI is at war. Anthropic and the Pentagon feuded over how to weaponize Anthropic’s AI model Claude; then OpenAI swept the Pentagon off its feet with an “opportunistic and sloppy” deal. Users quit ChatGPT in droves. People marched through London in the biggest protest against AI to date. If you’re...

item.title: Issues passing Challenge Pages and Turnstile
item.description: Mar 25, 08:39 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 25, 07:58 UTCInvestigating - Visitors encountering Turnstile or Cloudflare Challenge Pages are encountering intermittent issues solving the challenge and may have to retry.

item.title: HackerOne Employee Data Exposed in Massive Navia Breach
item.description: The cybersecurity firm said the personal information of hundreds of employees was stolen in the hacker attack targeting Navia. The post HackerOne Employee Data Exposed in Massive Navia Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Meizu Advances Flyme Ecosystem Partnerships Following Strategic Shift to AI
item.description: Meizu is accelerating partnerships around its Flyme ecosystem as it pivots from hardware to an AI-driven software strategy.

item.title: Meituan Responds to Photo Deletion Reports, Blames Android System Glitch
item.description: Meituan says a rare Android system conflict—not data access—caused reported photo deletions affecting a limited number of users.

item.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over Supply Chain and Cyber Risk Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it was banning the import of new, foreign-made consumer routers, citing "unacceptable" risks to cyber and national security. The action was designed to safeguard Americans and the underlying communications networks the country...

item.title: Pop Mart Expands into Home Appliances, Blind Box Giant Pushes Diversification Strategy
item.description: Pop Mart is entering the home appliance market, with product launches set for next month as part of a broader diversification push.

item.title: Alibaba Cloud Fully Opens JVS Claw, No Invite Needed to Raise Your Own “Cloud Lobster”
item.description: Alibaba Cloud opens JVS Claw to all users, adding voice input, file space, and one-click upgrades to its “cloud lobster” assistant.

item.title: [AINews] Apple's War on Slop
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on the End of Sora, LiteLLM, AI2, and other not so happy news.

item.title: Memory Bear AI Memory Science Engine for Multimodal Affective Intelligence: A Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2603.22306v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Affective judgment in real interaction is rarely a purely local prediction problem. Emotional meaning often depends on prior trajectory, accumulated context, and multimodal evidence that may be weak, noisy, or incomplete at the current moment....

item.title: The Efficiency Attenuation Phenomenon: A Computational Challenge to the Language of Thought Hypothesis
item.description: arXiv:2603.22312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper computationally investigates whether thought requires a language-like format, as posited by the Language of Thought (LoT) hypothesis. We introduce the ``AI Private Language'' thought experiment: if two artificial agents develop an...

item.title: Dynamic Fusion-Aware Graph Convolutional Neural Network for Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2603.22345v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversations (MERC) aims to identify and understand the emotions expressed by speakers during utterance interaction from multiple modalities (e.g., text, audio, images, etc.). Existing studies have shown that GCN can...

item.title: Intelligence Inertia: Physical Principles and Applications
item.description: arXiv:2603.22347v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Landauer's principle establishes the fundamental thermodynamic floor for information erasure and Fisher Information provides a metric for local curvature in parameter space, these classical frameworks function effectively only as approximations...

item.title: Session Risk Memory (SRM): Temporal Authorization for Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates
item.description: arXiv:2603.22350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deterministic pre-execution safety gates evaluate whether individual agent actions are compatible with their assigned roles. While effective at per-action authorization, these systems are structurally blind to distributed attacks that decompose...

item.title: Beyond Hard Constraints: Budget-Conditioned Reachability For Safe Offline Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.22292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sequential decision making using Markov Decision Process underpins many realworld applications. Both model-based and model free methods have achieved strong results in these settings. However, real-world tasks must balance reward maximization with...

item.title: Efficient Embedding-based Synthetic Data Generation for Complex Reasoning Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2603.22294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthetic Data Generation (SDG), leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), has recently been recognized and broadly adopted as an effective approach to improve the performance of smaller but more resource and compute efficient LLMs through fine-tuning....

item.title: Between the Layers Lies the Truth: Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs Using Intra-Layer Local Information Scores
item.description: arXiv:2603.22299v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are often confidently wrong, making reliable uncertainty estimation (UE) essential. Output-based heuristics are cheap but brittle, while probing internal representations is effective yet high-dimensional and hard to...

item.title: Scaling Attention via Feature Sparsity
item.description: arXiv:2603.22300v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scaling Transformers to ultra-long contexts is bottlenecked by the $O(n^2 d)$ cost of self-attention. Existing methods reduce this cost along the sequence axis through local windows, kernel approximations, or token-level sparsity, but these approaches...

item.title: Latent Semantic Manifolds in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.22301v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform internal computations in continuous vector spaces yet produce discrete tokens -- a fundamental mismatch whose geometric consequences remain poorly understood. We develop a mathematical framework that interprets LLM...

item.title: Evaluating Prompting Strategies for Chart Question Answering with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.22288v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prompting strategies affect LLM reasoning performance, but their role in chart-based QA remains underexplored. We present a systematic evaluation of four widely used prompting paradigms (Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought, and Few-Shot...

item.title: MERIT: Memory-Enhanced Retrieval for Interpretable Knowledge Tracing
item.description: arXiv:2603.22289v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge Tracing (KT) models students' evolving knowledge states to predict future performance, serving as a foundation for personalized education. While traditional deep learning models achieve high accuracy, they often lack interpretability. Large...

item.title: Less is More: Adapting Text Embeddings for Low-Resource Languages with Small Scale Noisy Synthetic Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.22290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-resource languages (LRLs) often lack high-quality, large-scale datasets for training effective text embedding models, hindering their application in tasks like retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and semantic search. In this work, we challenge...

item.title: Evaluating Large Language Models' Responses to Sexual and Reproductive Health Queries in Nepali
item.description: arXiv:2603.22291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) become integrated into daily life, they are increasingly used for personal queries, including Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), allowing users to chat anonymously without fear of judgment. However, current...

item.title: TIPS: Turn-Level Information-Potential Reward Shaping for Search-Augmented LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.22293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Search-augmented large language models (LLMs) trained with reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved strong results on open-domain question answering (QA), but training still remains a significant challenge. The optimization is often unstable due to...

item.title: JD.com Reveals Latest AI Progress Across Models, Digital Humans, and Embodied Intelligence
item.description: JD.com is scaling AI from open-source models to real-world data collection, aiming to lead in both digital and embodied intelligence.

item.title: Unable to download or preview project files
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance) Files (Operational)

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 24, 20:38 UTCUpdate - We are investigating elevated error rates affecting multiple GitHub services including Actions, Issues, Pull Requests, Webhooks, Codespaces, and login functionality. Some users may have experienced errors when accessing these features. Most services are now showing signs...

item.title: Elevated error rates on Realtime API SIP endpoints
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Realtime (Operational)

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Osaka, Japan datacenter (KIX)
item.description: Mar 24, 18:32 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 24, 18:13 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Network Performance in Osaka, Japan datacenter (KIX).We are working to analyze and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 Likely via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential harvester, a Kubernetes lateral movement toolkit, and a persistent backdoor. Multiple security vendors,...

item.title: Exclusive eBook: Are we ready to hand AI agents the keys?
item.description: We’re starting to give AI agents real autonomy, but are we prepared for what could happen next? This subscriber-only eBook explores this and angles from experts, such as “If we continue on the current path … we are basically playing Russian roulette with humanity.” by Grace Huckins June 12, 2025...

item.title: 3DCity-LLM: Empowering Multi-modality Large Language Models for 3D City-scale Perception and Understanding
item.description: While multi-modality large language models excel in object-centric or indoor scenarios, scaling them to 3D city-scale environments remains a formidable challenge. To bridge this gap, we propose 3DCity-LLM, a unified framework designed for 3D city-scale vision-language perception and understanding....

item.title: Similarity-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Data-Efficient Continual Learning
item.description: Machine learning models often need to adapt to new data after deployment due to structured or unstructured real-world dynamics. The Continual Learning (CL) framework enables continuous model adaptation, but most existing approaches either assume each task contains sufficiently many data samples or...

item.title: Teams Github Notifications App is down
item.description: Mar 24, 16:59 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: 🔬Why There Is No "AlphaFold for Materials" — AI for Materials Discovery with Heather Kulik
item.description: Lessons from a Decade on the Frontier of AI for Science

item.title: This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain
item.description: L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits cushioned in a vat at a storage facility in Arizona. It has been held there at a temperature of around −146 degrees °C for over a decade, largely undisturbed. That is, apart from the time, a little over a year ago, when scientists slowly lifted the brain to take...

item.title: SIMART: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-ready Articulated Assets via MLLM
item.description: High-quality articulated 3D assets are indispensable for embodied AI and physical simulation, yet 3D generation still focuses on static meshes, leaving a gap in "sim-ready" interactive objects. Most recent articulated object creation methods rely on multi-stage pipelines that accumulate errors...
